{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries","micro"],"rationale":"Use a stronger three-stage residual backbone, AdamW, OneCycle scheduling, and mild label smoothing to improve fixed-step CIFAR-10 validation loss while staying within the existing script shape.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
DEPTH = 1
BASE_CHANNELS = 8
CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True
DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0
FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"# --- Architecture hyperparameters -------------------------------------------
DEPTH = 2
BASE_CHANNELS = 16
CHANNEL_MULT = 2
USE_BATCHNORM = True
DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0
FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"# --- Optimizer hyperparameters ----------------------------------------------
OPTIMIZER = "adam"
LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4
WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4
MOMENTUM = 0.9
ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"# --- Optimizer hyperparameters ----------------------------------------------
OPTIMIZER = "adamw"
LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3
WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5
MOMENTUM = 0.9
ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        mid_channels = channels * CHANNEL_MULT
        out_channels = mid_channels * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM

        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        stage1_layers: list[nn.Module] = [
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        ]
        stage1_layers.extend(
            [ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(DEPTH)]
        )
        self.stage1 = nn.Sequential(*stage1_layers)

        down1_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, mid_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            down1_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(mid_channels))
        down1_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.down1 = nn.Sequential(*down1_layers)

        self.stage2 = nn.Sequential(
            *[ResidualBlock(mid_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(DEPTH)]
        )

        down2_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(mid_channels, out_channels, 3, stride=2, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            down2_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
        down2_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.down2 = nn.Sequential(*down2_layers)

        self.stage3 = nn.Sequential(
            *[ResidualBlock(out_channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(DEPTH)]
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(out_channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.stage1(x)
        x = self.down1(x)
        x = self.stage2(x)
        x = self.down2(x)
        x = self.stage3(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps)","new":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else max(1, steps_per_epoch * 4)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=0.2,
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
    )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.03)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The current run still looks somewhat under-trained, so a simple increase in the OneCycle max learning rate is the lowest-risk next move to push convergence harder without changing the model or data path.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["summaries"],"rationale":"The higher max LR overshot, so back off to a middle value to keep the faster schedule but reduce optimization noise.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The current model appears to benefit more from the earlier 1e-3 learning rate, and the fixed-step regime should profit from larger batches that expose more data per optimizer update.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Batch 256 clearly helped convergence, but the schedule is probably still a bit conservative; nudge the max learning rate up modestly to keep the larger-batch run moving.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.25e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The batch-256 run is still likely learning-rate limited, so probe a slightly higher max LR while keeping the rest of the recipe unchanged.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.25e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The batch-256 configuration still looks slightly under-accelerated, so nudge the OneCycle peak a bit higher to see whether convergence improves further.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.55e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The latest increase overshot, so retreat slightly while staying above the last better setting to probe the local optimum around the current batch-256 recipe.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.55e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.45e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The response seems to peak near 1.4e-3, so take a small downward step to search the narrower optimum window.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.45e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.375e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The learning-rate sweep looks close to its local optimum, so try a modest depth bump to increase representational capacity without changing the rest of the training recipe.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"layout","declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"],"rationale":"Undo the depth-3 regression, restore the better 1.4e-3 learning rate, and lower weight decay a bit to see whether the batch-256 recipe is still slightly under-regularized.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 2","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.375e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"Restore the better regularization setting and slightly soften label smoothing to see if the batch-256 recipe is still under-confident at validation time.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.03)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.02)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The restored regularization looks sound; trim label smoothing a bit further because the model may still be slightly over-smoothed.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.02)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.01)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The smoothing sweep is trending down, so try plain cross-entropy to remove the remaining regularization and see if validation loss keeps improving.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.01)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.0)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The no-smoothing run moved past the optimum, so reintroduce a small amount of smoothing between 0 and 0.01.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.0)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.005)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The 0.01 label-smoothing setting was the best performer so far; move back to that point rather than continuing to reduce regularization.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.005)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.01)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The recipe is near its local optimum; the remaining likely gain in fixed-step training is to expose more data to each optimizer update by increasing the batch size.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"micro","declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["topk"],"rationale":"The 384-batch run improved sharply, so push the fixed-step regime further by giving each optimizer step even more data.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"topk","declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The batch-512 configuration is the current best; a small upward LR probe is the lowest-cost way to test whether the optimum moved higher under the larger batch.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}

{"primary_mode":"caching","declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","secondary_modes":["micro"],"rationale":"The current best uses 0.01 label smoothing; a slightly lower value is the most conservative final probe around that local optimum.","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.01)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.008)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}]}
